verses.text: If a man steals an ox or sheep and kills it, he restores five oxen for an ox, four sheep for a sheep. A thief struck dead breaking in at night incurs no blood-guilt; by day, blood is shed. He makes full restitution; if penniless, sold for his theft. If found alive in his hand, he restores double. Guidance: Restitution scales with the harm. God's law makes repair the goal, not punishment alone.

He makes full restitution; if penniless, sold for his theft. If found alive in his hand, he restores double. Guidance: Restitution scales with the harm. God's law makes repair the goal, not punishment alone.

He makes full restitution; if penniless, sold for his theft. If found alive in his hand, he restores double. Guidance: Restitution scales with the harm. If a man lets his beast feed in another's field, or fire catches in thorns and consumes stacks of corn — he that kindled it shall make restitution from the best of his own. Guidance: Damage by what you set in motion — animal or fire — is your responsibility, even when unintended. God's law builds accountability for indirect harm.

If a man lets his beast feed in another's field, or fire catches in thorns and consumes stacks of corn — he that kindled it shall make restitution from the best of his own. Guidance: Damage by what you set in motion — animal or fire — is your responsibility, even when unintended. God's law builds accountability for indirect harm.

If a man delivers money or goods to his neighbor for safekeeping and they are stolen — the thief, if found, pays double. If not found, the householder is brought to the judges to see if he stole from his neighbor. For all trespass, the cause comes before the judges; whom they condemn pays double. Guidance: Disputes go to judges; both sides get a hearing. God refuses to leave trust without recourse.

Guidance: Disputes go to judges; both sides get a hearing. God refuses to leave trust without recourse.

If not found, the householder is brought to the judges to see if he stole from his neighbor. For all trespass, the cause comes before the judges; whom they condemn pays double. Guidance: Disputes go to judges; both sides get a hearing. God refuses to leave trust without recourse.

If a man delivers a beast to his neighbor to keep, and it dies or is hurt with no witness — an oath of the LORD shall be between them; the owner shall accept. If stolen, he shall restore. If torn, let him bring it for witness; he shall not restore. Guidance: Oaths before the LORD settle what witnesses cannot. Sometimes there is no proof — God's name closes the case.

If a man borrows from his neighbor and it is hurt or dies, the owner not being with it — he shall make restitution. But if the owner was with it, no restitution; if hired, it came for its hire. Guidance: When you borrow, you bear the risk; when the owner is present, the loss is shared. God's law honors both parties of a loan.

If a man borrows from his neighbor and it is hurt or dies, the owner not being with it — he shall make restitution. But if the owner was with it, no restitution; if hired, it came for its hire. Guidance: When you borrow, you bear the risk; when the owner is present, the loss is shared. God's law honors both parties of a loan.

If a man entice an unbetrothed maid and lie with her, he shall endow her as his wife. If her father refuse, he shall pay according to the dowry of virgins. Guidance: God's law requires the man who took her body to bear lifelong responsibility — or pay the full dowry if the father judges marriage unwise. The woman's honor and future are not left in his hands.

If a man entice an unbetrothed maid and lie with her, he shall endow her as his wife. If her father refuse, he shall pay according to the dowry of virgins. Guidance: God's law requires the man who took her body to bear lifelong responsibility — or pay the full dowry if the father judges marriage unwise. The woman's honor and future are not left in his hands.

Thou shalt not suffer a witch to live. Guidance: Witchcraft in the ancient world was the active courting of forbidden spiritual power. The death penalty signals how seriously God treats the choice to seek another voice than His.

Whosoever lieth with a beast shall surely be put to death. Guidance: Sexual sin that breaks the boundary between human and animal violates the order God built into creation itself. Some boundaries are non-negotiable.

He that sacrificeth unto any god, save unto the LORD only, he shall be utterly destroyed. Guidance: Worshiping another god is treated as covenant treason. Israel's existence depends on her loyalty to the One who delivered her. There is no second column.

Thou shalt neither vex a stranger nor oppress him — for ye were strangers in Egypt. Ye shall not afflict any widow or fatherless child. If thou afflict them, and they cry to Me, I will surely hear; My wrath shall wax hot, I will kill you with the sword. Guidance: God identifies personally with the foreigner, the widow, the fatherless. He hears their cry — and what He hears, He answers. This law is the heart of the prophets.

If thou lend money to My poor people, be not a usurer to him. If thou take thy neighbor's raiment to pledge, deliver it by sunset: that is his only covering. When he crieth to Me, I will hear; for I am gracious. Guidance: Profit from the poor by interest is forbidden. A cloak taken in pledge must come back before nightfall — because he sleeps under it. God's law presses lenders to remember the body of the borrower.

When he crieth to Me, I will hear; for I am gracious. Guidance: Profit from the poor by interest is forbidden. A cloak taken in pledge must come back before nightfall — because he sleeps under it. Thou shalt not revile the gods, nor curse the ruler of thy people. Guidance: The Hebrew word for gods here likely refers to the judges of Israel — those exercising God-given authority. Speak respectfully of those God has placed over you, even when you disagree.

Thou shalt not delay to offer the first of thy ripe fruits and thy liquors. The firstborn of thy sons shalt thou give unto Me. Likewise with thine oxen and sheep: seven days it shall be with its dam; on the eighth day, give it Me. Guidance: The first goes to God — fruit, child, animal. Not the leftovers, the first. God protects His claim on the firstfruits because what we give first reveals what we trust.

And ye shall be holy men unto Me: neither shall ye eat any flesh that is torn of beasts in the field; ye shall cast it to the dogs. Guidance: Holiness is even about what you eat. Even scavenged meat — what a wild animal killed and left — is not for God's people. Eat what is given cleanly, not what is found bleeding.
